reasoning: At 1x the sword reads as a sword shape but is extremely thin — a few bright pixels — making it marginal for instant recognition without color cues. At 4x silhouette clarity is solid: the T-crossguard gives a readable iconic form and the ice-blue palette with white pixel highlights contrasts well on both dark and the cream light background. However the charm criterion is the weak point. A straight symmetrical sword is inherently an aggressive/edgy form — no round or soft elements, no visual personality beyond 'weapon'. The ice-blue coloring adds some visual interest and a degree of 'cool toy' read, but there is no friendly gesture, no quirky proportion, no sticker-worthy personality. The asset clears the silhouette bar at 4x but falls short of the cute/charm bar. Charming-but-mushy does not apply here; clear-but-charmless does.
